title: pterocarpus lucens subsp. antunesii
intro: Pterocarpus lucens subsp. antunesii, also known as the Mozambican Pterocarpus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of southern Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Pterocarpus lucens subsp. antunesii include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate digestive issues. In traditional herbal medicine, it is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its bark, leaves, and roots are used to make various herbal preparations, including tonics, ointments, and teas.
